answer:
\boxed{A}  
The Paul-Bunnell test detects heterophile antibodies, which are produced in infectious mononucleosis and agglutinate sheep or horse red blood cells. This distinguishes it from other agglutination tests, as it relies on cross-reacting antibodies not specific to the Epstein-Barr virus itself. Thus, it is classified as a heterophile antibody test.
